concepts.title: 用 `rg` 快速定位代码
concepts.lesson: `rg` 会在项目文件中寻找符合条件的文字。它像一位熟悉档案柜位置的助手：先告诉你相关内容在哪，再决定要不要打开那一页。这样排查问题时不必靠记忆翻遍整个项目。
concepts.question: 运行 `rg` 最直接帮助了什么？
options: 快速找出可能相关的位置，再决定查看哪里。
自动修改所有找到的内容。
把所有文件打包成一个新项目。
concepts.explanation: 对。`rg` 的职责是搜索和定位；是否修改、如何修改，仍要由后续步骤决定。

en.title: Find code quickly with `rg`
en.lesson: `rg` searches project files for matching text. It is like an assistant who knows where the filing cabinets are: it points to relevant places before you decide which page to open. That means you do not need to search the whole project from memory.
en.question: What does running `rg` help with most directly?
options: Find likely relevant places, then decide what to inspect.
Automatically change every result it finds.
Package every file into a new project.
en.explanation: Correct. `rg` searches and locates; later steps still decide whether and how to change anything.

concepts.title: 让 `node --test` 检查行为
concepts.lesson: `node --test` 会按事先写好的小问题检查程序行为。它像出门前逐项核对清单：不是证明一切完美，而是尽早发现某个已知承诺被改坏了。
concepts.question: `node --test` 最像下面哪件事？
options: 按清单检查重要行为是否仍然成立。
把项目自动发布给所有人。
把所有测试内容翻译成另一种语言。
concepts.explanation: 对。测试把重要预期写成可重复的检查，修改后能迅速确认它们是否还成立。

en.title: Use `node --test` to check behavior
en.lesson: `node --test` checks program behavior against small questions written in advance. It is like a checklist before leaving home: it does not prove everything is perfect, but it can quickly reveal when a known promise was broken.
en.question: What is `node --test` most like?
options: Checking whether important behavior still holds against a checklist.
Automatically publishing the project for everyone.
Translating every test into another language.
en.explanation: Correct. Tests turn important expectations into repeatable checks, so a change can be checked quickly.

concepts.title: 用 `npm` 准备项目依赖
concepts.lesson: 项目会把需要借用的工具列成清单。`npm` 根据这份清单把合适版本准备到本地，像开工前按配方备齐原料；它不会替你决定菜要怎么做。
concepts.question: 安装依赖主要是在做什么？
options: 按项目清单准备运行和开发所需的工具。
把项目中的所有问题自动修好。
把当前代码永久锁定不能再改。
concepts.explanation: 对。依赖安装是在准备工具和版本，而不是替代设计、调试或提交代码。

en.title: Prepare project dependencies with `npm`
en.lesson: A project lists the tools it needs to borrow. `npm` prepares suitable versions locally from that list, like gathering ingredients from a recipe before cooking. It does not decide how the dish should be made.
en.question: What is dependency installation mainly doing?
options: Preparing the tools needed to run and develop the project from its list.
Automatically fixing every problem in the project.
Permanently locking the current code so it cannot change.
en.explanation: Correct. Installing dependencies prepares tools and versions; it does not replace design, debugging, or committing code.

concepts.title: 通过 `npm run` 复用项目流程
concepts.lesson: `npm run` 把常用操作起了统一名字。它像厨房里的预设按钮：按下同一个按钮，每个人都会执行同一套步骤，减少口头约定和手工输入造成的偏差。
concepts.question: 项目脚本的主要价值是什么？
options: 让重复操作以统一方式被执行。
让任何命令都不需要配置。
让程序不再需要测试。
concepts.explanation: 对。脚本把重复流程固定下来，团队成员和自动化工具都能使用相同入口。

en.title: Reuse project workflows with `npm run`
en.lesson: `npm run` gives common operations shared names. It is like a preset button in a kitchen: pressing the same button runs the same steps for everyone, reducing drift from verbal instructions and manual typing.
en.question: What is the main value of a project script?
options: It runs repeated work in a consistent way.
It means no command ever needs configuration.
It means the program no longer needs tests.
en.explanation: Correct. Scripts standardize repeated workflows so people and automation use the same entry point.

concepts.title: 用 `git status` 看清改动状态
concepts.lesson: `git status` 像收拾行李前的清点单：哪些东西刚放进去、哪些还没装、哪些已经准备好交出去，一眼就能分清。它不改变内容，只说明现在处于什么状态。
concepts.question: `git status` 最适合回答什么问题？
options: 当前有哪些改动，以及它们准备到了哪一步。
哪个改动一定没有错误。
下一次发布会在什么时候发生。
concepts.explanation: 对。它提供工作区状态，不会替人判断代码质量或安排发布。

en.title: See change status with `git status`
en.lesson: `git status` is like an inventory before packing: what was just added, what is still unpacked, and what is ready to hand over. It changes nothing; it only tells you the current state.
en.question: What question is `git status` best suited to answer?
options: What has changed now, and how far along each change is.
Which change definitely has no errors.
When the next release will happen.
en.explanation: Correct. It reports working-tree state; it does not judge code quality or schedule a release.

concepts.title: 用 `git diff` 对照改了什么
concepts.lesson: `git diff` 把修改前后并排比较，像校对稿上的增删标记。它让人把注意力放在变化本身，而不是重新阅读全部内容。
concepts.question: 为什么在提交前查看差异很有用？
options: 能集中检查这次实际改变的部分。
能让改动自动通过测试。
能把所有旧版本立即删除。
concepts.explanation: 对。差异视图缩小了检查范围，但仍需要人或测试判断改动是否正确。

en.title: Compare what changed with `git diff`
en.lesson: `git diff` compares before and after side by side, like the addition and deletion marks in a proofread draft. It focuses attention on the change itself instead of making you reread everything.
en.question: Why is reviewing a diff useful before committing?
options: It focuses review on the parts that actually changed.
It makes changes pass tests automatically.
It immediately deletes every older version.
en.explanation: Correct. A diff narrows the review surface, but people or tests still judge whether the change is right.

concepts.title: 用 `git commit` 留下可追溯存档
concepts.lesson: `git commit` 会把一组已经选定的改动记录成一个有说明的存档点。它像给完成的一小段工作贴上日期和标签，日后能知道当时为什么这样改。
concepts.question: 一次提交最重要的作用是什么？
options: 把一组相关改动保存为可追溯的记录。
让程序自动部署到线上。
让其他改动从此无法发生。
concepts.explanation: 对。提交提供历史和边界；发布、协作和后续修改仍是独立步骤。

en.title: Create a traceable record with `git commit`
en.lesson: `git commit` records a selected group of changes as a documented save point. It is like putting a date and label on one finished piece of work so you can later understand why it changed.
en.question: What is the most important role of a commit?
options: Save related changes as a traceable record.
Deploy the program to production automatically.
Prevent any future changes from happening.
en.explanation: Correct. A commit provides history and a boundary; release, collaboration, and later edits are separate steps.

concepts.title: 切换分支来隔离工作线
concepts.lesson: 分支像同一份地图上的不同透明图层。切换分支可以先查看或继续另一条工作线，而不会把两条尚未整理好的路线混在一起。
concepts.question: 分支切换主要帮助避免什么？
options: 把不同工作线的未整理改动混在一起。
任何人再也看不到历史记录。
项目需要使用版本控制。
concepts.explanation: 对。分支帮助隔离不同方向的工作，同时仍保留共同的历史基础。

en.title: Switch branches to separate lines of work
en.lesson: Branches are like different transparent layers on the same map. Switching branches lets you inspect or continue another line of work without mixing two routes that are not yet organized together.
en.question: What does switching branches mainly help avoid?
options: Mixing unfinished changes from different lines of work together.
Anyone ever seeing the history again.
The project needing version control.
en.explanation: Correct. Branches isolate work in different directions while preserving their shared history.

concepts.title: 用 `node --watch` 跟随文件变化
concepts.lesson: `node --watch` 会留意指定文件是否变化，并在变化后重新开始相关工作。它像值班员听见门铃就重新检查，而不是让人每次修改后手动提醒。
concepts.question: 监听模式减少了哪一种重复？
options: 每次修改后手动重新启动同一项检查。
每次修改前先写测试。
每次提交前查看历史。
concepts.explanation: 对。监听模式自动响应变化；它不会代替对结果的判断。

en.title: Follow file changes with `node --watch`
en.lesson: `node --watch` notices when selected files change and restarts related work. It is like an attendant who checks again when a bell rings instead of needing a person to remind them after every edit.
en.question: What repetition does watch mode reduce?
options: Manually restarting the same check after every edit.
Writing tests before every edit.
Reviewing history before every commit.
en.explanation: Correct. Watch mode responds to changes automatically; it does not replace judging the result.

concepts.title: 让静态检查提前发现常见问题
concepts.lesson: 静态检查会在程序真正运行前查看结构和常见约定。它像交稿前的格式检查员，能抓住不少低成本的问题，却不能替代对实际功能的验证。
concepts.question: 静态检查最擅长发现哪类问题？
options: 结构和约定中能提前识别的常见问题。
所有用户在未来会提出的需求。
线上环境一定发生过的故障。
concepts.explanation: 对。静态检查覆盖的是可从代码形状判断的一部分问题，功能验证仍然重要。

en.title: Use static checks to catch common issues early
en.lesson: Static checks inspect structure and common conventions before a program runs. They are like a format reviewer before a submission: they catch many low-cost issues, but they do not replace checking real behavior.
en.question: What kind of issue are static checks best at finding?
options: Common issues visible early in structure and conventions.
Every need users will raise in the future.
Failures that definitely happened in production.
en.explanation: Correct. Static checks cover problems visible from code shape; functional verification still matters.

concepts.title: 用格式化工具统一代码外观
concepts.lesson: 格式化工具把缩进、换行和空格整理成一致样子。它像把同一份表格按统一规则排版，让阅读者把注意力放在内容，而不是每个人不同的排版习惯。
concepts.question: 格式化工具主要改变什么？
options: 代码的排版和外观一致性。
程序要实现的业务目标。
依赖包的授权方式。
concepts.explanation: 对。格式化通常不改变程序意图，重点是让协作时的阅读成本更低。

en.title: Use a formatter for consistent code appearance
en.lesson: A formatter makes indentation, line breaks, and spacing consistent. It is like laying out the same table by one shared rule, so readers can focus on content instead of each person's formatting habits.
en.question: What does a formatting tool mainly change?
options: The consistency of code layout and appearance.
The business goal the program must achieve.
The license of a dependency package.
en.explanation: Correct. Formatting usually does not change program intent; it lowers the cost of reading together.

concepts.title: 在浏览器中打开真实页面
concepts.lesson: 打开页面是把程序结果放到真实使用环境里看一眼。它像把舞台布景真正点亮，而不只是在图纸上判断；有些问题只有运行后才会出现。
concepts.question: 为什么需要在浏览器中查看页面？
options: 有些视觉和交互问题只有运行后才看得见。
浏览器会自动替项目写完功能。
打开页面会让测试不再需要。
concepts.explanation: 对。运行中的页面提供真实反馈，但仍需要结合测试和检查来判断结果。

en.title: Open a real page in the browser
en.lesson: Opening a page lets you see the program's result in its real use environment. It is like turning on a stage set instead of judging it only from a drawing; some problems appear only after it runs.
en.question: Why view a page in the browser?
options: Some visual and interaction problems appear only after it runs.
The browser automatically writes the remaining feature.
Opening the page means tests are no longer needed.
en.explanation: Correct. A running page gives real feedback, but tests and inspection are still needed to judge the result.

concepts.title: 用截图留下视觉检查证据
concepts.lesson: 截图把某一刻的页面状态固定下来，像给现场拍一张可回看的照片。它方便比较修改前后，也能让不在现场的人理解当时实际看到了什么。
concepts.question: 截图最适合作为哪一种材料？
options: 某一时刻页面外观的可回看证据。
保证所有设备显示完全相同的承诺。
替代所有交互操作的记录。
concepts.explanation: 对。截图记录一个视觉瞬间；不同设备和后续交互仍可能呈现不同结果。

en.title: Keep visual-check evidence with a screenshot
en.lesson: A screenshot fixes one moment of a page in place, like a photo you can revisit. It helps compare before and after, and lets someone who was not present understand what was actually visible.
en.question: What is a screenshot best used as?
options: Revisitable evidence of how a page looked at one moment.
A promise that every device will look exactly the same.
A replacement for every record of interaction.
en.explanation: Correct. A screenshot records one visual moment; different devices and later interactions can still differ.

concepts.title: 通过点击验证交互路径
concepts.lesson: 点击是在模拟用户沿着某条路径使用界面。它像亲手按下门铃确认铃会响：静态画面看起来正确，不等于操作后真的有预期反应。
concepts.question: 点击检查补上了静态页面缺少的什么信息？
options: 用户操作后界面是否做出预期反应。
页面文件的全部历史。
服务器硬件的购买时间。
concepts.explanation: 对。交互检查关注动作和反馈之间的连接，而不仅是页面的初始外观。

en.title: Verify an interaction path by clicking
en.lesson: Clicking simulates a user taking a path through an interface. It is like ringing a doorbell yourself to confirm it rings: a static screen can look right without responding as expected after an action.
en.question: What information does a click check add beyond a static page?
options: Whether the interface reacts as expected after a user action.
The complete history of the page file.
When the server hardware was purchased.
en.explanation: Correct. Interaction checks connect an action to its feedback, not only the page's initial appearance.

concepts.title: 先读取文件再判断改动
concepts.lesson: 读取文件是在动手前确认现状。它像修理前先打开说明书和查看零件位置，避免凭猜测改到不该动的地方。
concepts.question: 先读取相关文件的价值是什么？
options: 根据真实现状做判断，而不是凭猜测修改。
让文件从此不能再被修改。
自动把所有文件合并成一个。
concepts.explanation: 对。理解当前内容能减少误改风险，但不等于已经完成修复。

en.title: Read files before deciding on a change
en.lesson: Reading files confirms the current situation before acting. It is like opening instructions and checking where parts are before a repair, so you do not change the wrong thing based on a guess.
en.question: What is the value of reading relevant files first?
options: Make decisions from the real situation instead of changing things by guesswork.
Make the files impossible to modify from then on.
Automatically combine every file into one.
en.explanation: Correct. Understanding current content reduces the risk of a wrong edit, but it does not mean the fix is complete.

concepts.title: 通过评审理解改动边界
concepts.lesson: 评审把改动放到另一个人的视角下检查。它像请同伴沿着你刚走过的路线再走一遍，常能发现自己因为太熟悉而忽略的岔路。
concepts.question: 评审最有价值的地方是什么？
options: 引入不同视角来检查改动的边界和影响。
保证任何改动都不再需要测试。
让所有人必须采用同一种写法。
concepts.explanation: 对。评审增加独立检查，不是自动保证正确性的魔法步骤。

en.title: Understand a change boundary through review
en.lesson: Review examines a change from another person's perspective. It is like asking a teammate to walk the route you just took; they can often spot turns you missed because the route was too familiar.
en.question: What is most valuable about review?
options: Bringing another perspective to a change's boundaries and effects.
Guaranteeing that no change ever needs testing again.
Making everyone use exactly one writing style.
en.explanation: Correct. Review adds an independent check; it is not a magic step that guarantees correctness.

concepts.title: 理解脚本逻辑的改动
concepts.lesson: 脚本文件常用来描述程序在收到输入后应如何行动。修改它像调整一份流程说明：同一个按钮被按下时，后面会走向不同的步骤。
concepts.question: 脚本逻辑改动最可能影响什么？
options: 程序收到输入后采取的步骤。
显示器的物理亮度。
项目文件夹的创建日期。
concepts.explanation: 对。脚本通常描述行为流程，因此改动需要结合输入和结果一起检查。

en.title: Understand a script-logic change
en.lesson: Script files often describe how a program should act after receiving input. Changing one is like adjusting a process description: pressing the same button can lead to different later steps.
en.question: What is a script-logic change most likely to affect?
options: The steps a program takes after receiving input.
The physical brightness of a monitor.
The date the project folder was created.
en.explanation: Correct. Scripts usually describe behavior flow, so a change should be checked with both inputs and outcomes.

concepts.title: 用类型约束表达数据形状
concepts.lesson: 类型像给数据贴上的形状标签：这里应放数字、那里应放姓名或一组项目。它让工具在运行前就能发现一些“盒子里装错东西”的问题。
concepts.question: 类型约束最直接帮助防止什么？
options: 把不符合预期形状的数据放进错误位置。
所有界面都自动变得更漂亮。
所有网络请求都自动成功。
concepts.explanation: 对。类型能提前发现一类数据不匹配问题，但不能覆盖所有运行时情况。

en.title: Use types to describe data shape
en.lesson: Types are like shape labels on data: a number belongs here, a name or list belongs there. They let tools catch some cases of putting the wrong thing in a box before the program runs.
en.question: What do type constraints most directly help prevent?
options: Putting data with the wrong expected shape in the wrong place.
Making every interface automatically prettier.
Making every network request succeed automatically.
en.explanation: Correct. Types can catch one class of data mismatch early, but they do not cover every runtime situation.

concepts.title: 用 `jq` 筛选结构化数据
concepts.lesson: `jq` 会按明确条件从结构化数据中挑出需要的部分。它像在一叠格式统一的表单里勾选符合条件的项目，不必靠肉眼逐条翻找。
concepts.question: 使用 `jq` 最直接减少了什么工作？
options: 从结构化数据中手工逐条寻找目标。
把所有数据自动发布出去。
让数据从此不再需要检查。
concepts.explanation: 对。 `jq` 帮你按条件筛选；条件是否合适仍需要人确认。

en.title: Filter structured data with `jq`
en.lesson: `jq` selects the needed parts of structured data using explicit conditions. It is like checking matching items from a stack of consistently formatted forms instead of searching through them one by one.
en.question: What work does using `jq` most directly reduce?
options: Manually looking through structured data one item at a time.
Automatically publishing all data.
Making data never need checking again.
en.explanation: Correct. `jq` filters by conditions; a person still needs to confirm that the conditions are appropriate.

concepts.title: 用 `tmux` 保持多个终端工作区
concepts.lesson: `tmux` 可以把多个终端会话整理在一个可恢复的工作区里。它像把几张正在使用的工作台排进同一间房，暂时离开后还能回到原来的位置。
concepts.question: `tmux` 最适合帮助保留什么？
options: 多项终端工作的上下文和位置。
每个命令永远都成功。
项目不再需要版本记录。
concepts.explanation: 对。 `tmux` 组织和恢复终端会话，不会替你判断命令结果。

en.title: Keep multiple terminal workspaces with `tmux`
en.lesson: `tmux` organizes multiple terminal sessions in one recoverable workspace. It is like arranging several active workbenches in one room, so you can return to their places after stepping away.
en.question: What is `tmux` best at preserving?
options: The context and place of multiple terminal tasks.
A guarantee that every command will always succeed.
A project no longer needing version history.
en.explanation: Correct. `tmux` organizes and restores terminal sessions; it does not judge command results for you.

concepts.title: 用 `fetch` 取得网页资源
concepts.lesson: `fetch` 是向一个地址请求资源的动作。它像请资料员按地址取来一份材料：拿到材料之后，仍要检查来源、状态和内容是否符合需要。
concepts.question: `fetch` 完成后还需要做什么？
options: 检查返回的状态和内容是否可用。
假定每次请求都必然正确。
自动替网页写完界面。
concepts.explanation: 对。 `fetch` 负责请求；是否成功和如何处理结果需要后续判断。

en.title: Fetch web resources with `fetch`
en.lesson: `fetch` requests a resource from an address. It is like asking a records clerk to retrieve a document by address: after it arrives, you still need to check its source, status, and contents.
en.question: What still needs to happen after `fetch`?
options: Check whether the returned status and content are usable.
Assume every request must be correct.
Automatically finish writing the web interface.
en.explanation: Correct. `fetch` makes the request; later judgment determines whether it succeeded and how to handle the result.

concepts.title: 用 `SSE` 接收持续推送
concepts.lesson: `SSE` 让服务器可以持续把新消息推到浏览器或客户端。它像订阅一块会不断更新的公告板，而不是每次都重新跑去问有没有新通知。
concepts.question: `SSE` 最适合哪种信息流？
options: 服务器持续发送的新状态或通知。
一次性永久保存所有文件。
让网络连接永远不会中断。
concepts.explanation: 对。 `SSE` 适合单向持续更新；连接状态仍需要被妥善处理。

en.title: Receive ongoing updates with `SSE`
en.lesson: `SSE` lets a server keep sending new messages to a browser or client. It is like subscribing to a noticeboard that keeps updating instead of repeatedly asking whether there is a new notice.
en.question: What kind of information flow is `SSE` best for?
options: New state or notifications sent continuously by a server.
Saving every file permanently in a single action.
Guaranteeing that a network connection never breaks.
en.explanation: Correct. `SSE` suits one-way ongoing updates; connection state still needs proper handling.

concepts.title: 用 `grep` 按模式查找文字
concepts.lesson: `grep` 可以根据一套文字模式筛选内容。它像给查找条件写一张小规则卡：符合规则的留下，不符合的先略过。
concepts.question: 正则模式最直接决定什么？
options: 哪些文字会被视为匹配结果。
文件一定会被自动修改。
所有搜索结果一定正确。
concepts.explanation: 对。模式定义匹配边界，写完后仍要检查它是否覆盖了想找的内容。

en.title: Search text by pattern with `grep`
en.lesson: `grep` filters content using a text pattern. It is like writing a small rule card for a search: text that fits the rule stays, and other text is set aside for now.
en.question: What does a regular-expression pattern most directly determine?
options: Which text counts as a matching result.
That files will definitely be changed automatically.
That every search result is definitely correct.
en.explanation: Correct. A pattern defines the matching boundary; after writing it, you still check whether it covers what you meant to find.

concepts.title: 用 `CSS` 动画表达状态变化
concepts.lesson: `CSS` 动画把界面从一个状态平滑地过渡到另一个状态。它像给静态标志加上清楚的转场提示，让人更容易看懂变化正在发生。
concepts.question: 动画最适合补充哪一种信息？
options: 界面状态正在如何变化。
所有业务规则的正确答案。
网络请求一定成功的保证。
concepts.explanation: 对。 `CSS` 动画帮助表达变化；功能结果仍要通过真实交互和检查确认。

en.title: Use `CSS` animation to show state changes
en.lesson: `CSS` animation moves an interface smoothly from one state to another. It is like adding a clear transition cue to a static sign, making it easier to understand that a change is happening.
en.question: What kind of information does animation best add?
options: How an interface state is changing.
The correct answer to every business rule.
A guarantee that a network request will succeed.
en.explanation: Correct. `CSS` animation helps express change; real interaction and checking still confirm functional results.

concepts.title: 用样式规则安排页面外观
concepts.lesson: 样式规则决定文字、间距、颜色和布局如何呈现。它像给房间制定摆放规则：家具没有变，但位置、层次和舒适感会明显不同。
concepts.question: 样式改动主要影响什么？
options: 页面内容呈现出来的外观和布局。
数据在数据库中的历史。
版本控制的提交顺序。
concepts.explanation: 对。样式主要负责呈现，页面行为和数据处理通常由其他部分承担。

en.title: Arrange a page's appearance with style rules
en.lesson: Style rules determine how text, spacing, color, and layout appear. They are like rules for arranging a room: the furniture has not changed, but its position, hierarchy, and comfort can feel very different.
en.question: What do style changes mainly affect?
options: The appearance and layout of page content.
The history of data in a database.
The order of version-control commits.
en.explanation: Correct. Styles mainly control presentation; page behavior and data handling usually belong to other parts.

concepts.title: 用页面结构表达内容层级
concepts.lesson: 页面结构描述哪些内容是标题、段落、按钮或区域。它像搭建房屋骨架：先分清房间和通道，之后再安排装饰和互动。
concepts.question: 页面结构最先解决什么问题？
options: 内容之间的层级和组成关系。
每个用户的网络速度。
项目的发布日程。
concepts.explanation: 对。结构给内容一个清晰骨架，再由样式和行为让它完整呈现。

en.title: Use page structure to express content hierarchy
en.lesson: Page structure describes what is a heading, paragraph, button, or region. It is like building a house frame: first distinguish rooms and passages, then arrange decoration and interaction.
en.question: What problem does page structure solve first?
options: The hierarchy and composition between pieces of content.
Each user's network speed.
The project's release schedule.
en.explanation: Correct. Structure gives content a clear frame; styles and behavior then make the presentation complete.

concepts.title: 用结构化数据保存明确关系
concepts.lesson: 结构化数据把信息按固定字段组织起来，像一张填写格式统一的表单。程序因此能稳定地找到每一项，而不用从一大段描述里猜意思。
concepts.question: 结构化数据的关键好处是什么？
options: 让程序能按明确字段稳定读取信息。
让数据永远不需要更新。
让所有信息都必须公开。
concepts.explanation: 对。固定结构降低理解歧义，但数据仍需要正确维护和验证。

en.title: Store explicit relationships with structured data
en.lesson: Structured data organizes information into fixed fields, like a consistently filled form. A program can then find each item reliably instead of guessing meaning from one long description.
en.question: What is a key benefit of structured data?
options: It lets a program reliably read information from explicit fields.
It means data never needs updating.
It means every piece of information must be public.
en.explanation: Correct. A fixed structure reduces ambiguity, but the data still needs correct maintenance and validation.

concepts.title: 把决策写进项目说明
concepts.lesson: 说明文件把目标、用法和取舍留给未来的读者。它像给接手的人留下一张清楚的便签：不仅知道现在有什么，也知道为什么这样安排。
concepts.question: 项目说明最能帮助谁？
options: 未来需要理解项目的人，包括之后的自己。
只帮助已经记得全部细节的人。
自动替项目完成运行。
concepts.explanation: 对。说明把上下文保存下来，尤其能减少未来重新猜测决策原因的成本。

en.title: Write decisions into project documentation
en.lesson: Documentation preserves goals, use, and tradeoffs for future readers. It is like leaving a clear note for whoever takes over: they learn not only what exists now, but why it was arranged that way.
en.question: Who does project documentation help most?
options: People who need to understand the project later, including your future self.
Only people who already remember every detail.
Automatically completing the project's runtime work.
en.explanation: Correct. Documentation preserves context and especially reduces the cost of guessing later why a decision was made.

concepts.title: 通过脚本组织自动步骤
concepts.lesson: 脚本可以把一连串重复动作写成可重复执行的流程。它像把每天的手工步骤写成食谱：材料和顺序清楚后，别人也能按同样方式完成。
concepts.question: 自动脚本最适合减少什么？
options: 重复而容易漏步骤的手工操作。
所有需要人做判断的决定。
所有项目中的数据量。
concepts.explanation: 对。脚本擅长稳定执行已定义步骤，判断目标是否合理仍需要人参与。

en.title: Organize automated steps with a script
en.lesson: A script can turn a sequence of repeated actions into a repeatable process. It is like writing daily manual steps as a recipe: once ingredients and order are clear, someone else can complete the same process.
en.question: What are automation scripts best at reducing?
options: Repeated manual work where steps are easy to miss.
Every decision that needs human judgment.
The amount of data in every project.
en.explanation: Correct. Scripts reliably execute defined steps; people still judge whether the goal is appropriate.

concepts.title: 用查询描述想取得的数据
concepts.lesson: 查询是在向数据仓库说明你想要哪些记录、按什么条件筛选。它像在图书馆提出一张明确借书单：条件越清楚，取回的结果越接近所需。
concepts.question: 查询条件最直接决定什么？
options: 会被选出来的记录范围。
页面字体的大小。
版本提交的作者。
concepts.explanation: 对。条件决定筛选边界，因此修改查询时要特别留意是否扩大或缩小了结果。

en.title: Describe the data you want with a query
en.lesson: A query tells a data store which records you want and which conditions should filter them. It is like giving a library a clear borrowing list: clearer conditions bring returned results closer to what you need.
en.question: What do query conditions most directly determine?
options: The range of records that will be selected.
The size of a page's font.
The author of a version-control commit.
en.explanation: Correct. Conditions define the filter boundary, so changing a query can widen or narrow its results.

concepts.title: 把并发工作分成可协调任务
concepts.lesson: 并发让多项等待中的工作可以交错推进，像厨房里煮水时同时切菜。关键不是同时做得越多越好，而是让每项任务在合适时机交换结果。
concepts.question: 并发最适合处理哪种情形？
options: 多项会等待彼此不同资源的工作。
把一个错误隐藏起来不再显示。
让所有任务必然同时结束。
concepts.explanation: 对。并发能改善等待安排，但仍需要清楚协调顺序和共享资源。

en.title: Split concurrent work into coordinated tasks
en.lesson: Concurrency lets multiple waiting tasks make progress in an interleaved way, like chopping vegetables while water boils. The point is not to do everything at once, but to exchange results at the right time.
en.question: What situation is concurrency best suited to handle?
options: Multiple tasks waiting on different resources.
Hiding an error so it no longer appears.
Making every task definitely finish at the same time.
en.explanation: Correct. Concurrency can improve how waiting is arranged, but order and shared resources still need clear coordination.

concepts.title: 在编译前检查资源使用边界
concepts.lesson: 资源使用边界像借用物品时的登记规则：谁在使用、何时归还、能否同时交给别人，需要尽量在动手前说清。这样能减少运行时才暴露的混乱。
concepts.question: 提前检查资源边界想减少什么？
options: 多人或多步骤使用同一资源时的混乱。
所有程序的启动时间。
所有界面的视觉差异。
concepts.explanation: 对。提前约束资源使用能防住一类问题，但仍要验证整体行为是否符合需要。

en.title: Check resource-use boundaries before compiling
en.lesson: Resource-use boundaries are like borrowing rules: who is using something, when it returns, and whether it can be handed to someone else should be clear before work begins. That reduces confusion that would otherwise appear only at runtime.
en.question: What does checking resource boundaries early aim to reduce?
options: Confusion when people or steps use the same resource.
The startup time of every program.
Visual differences between every interface.
en.explanation: Correct. Early resource constraints prevent one class of issue, but overall behavior still needs verification.

concepts.title: 把命令步骤写成可重复流程
concepts.lesson: 命令流程写下来后，每次执行都会遵循相同顺序。它像把活动准备清单贴在墙上，既能减少遗漏，也让别人知道这套流程包含哪些环节。
concepts.question: 命令自动化最明显减少什么风险？
options: 重复执行时漏掉某个固定步骤。
任何外部服务都不可用。
所有项目都需要同一套工具。
concepts.explanation: 对。自动化让固定步骤更一致，但外部条件和业务判断仍要单独处理。

en.title: Turn command steps into a repeatable workflow
en.lesson: Once command steps are written down, each run follows the same order. It is like putting an event-preparation checklist on a wall: it reduces omissions and shows others which stages the workflow includes.
en.question: What risk does command automation most clearly reduce?
options: Missing a fixed step during repeated execution.
Any external service being unavailable.
Every project needing the same tools.
en.explanation: Correct. Automation makes fixed steps more consistent, but external conditions and business judgment still need separate handling.
